title: Image 2 of Phenix gazette (Alexandria [D.C.]), July 13, 1831
description: eCfiLISHED DAILY BY S AM UK L SNOWDEN OX FAIRFAX ST BETWEEN KIN3 AND PRINCE STS AS11 OH XUr DAYS THURSDAYS AD SATURDAYS OH THK COVWTHT xuy Paper S3 per annumContry Paper 85 WKdNESD CYJULY 15 1851 From the Charleston Courier CELEBRATION Of the Fiftyfifth Anniversary of American In dependence in Charleston S C The dawn was ushered in by the tiring of can non and the ringing of bells and throughout the day at brief intervals various patriotic tunes were chimed on the bells of St Michaels Church The Fourth Brigade under Gen Vandeihorst assembled at 5 oclock in the morning was re viewed by the Governor at 6 oclock marched to the Battery on East Bay and there tired a sa lute and feu de joiethey proceeded thence to South Bay and up Meeting street to the Brigade ground where the troops were dismissed For some days previous the weather had been wet aud disagreeable ami on the Fourth rain fell at intervals throughout the day Th nrcnovations for the celebration of the day nude bv the two great parties broufft out at an early hour nearly the whole male population of the citv and before 1Poclock masses
